column1: m4.1 As a User, I want to view my bank details

column2: Verify that the user can access bank details from home screen
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user only has one product.
column5:
1. Observe the details on the screen
2. Tap on Hide my Balance
3. Tap on the "View Account Details" button
column6:
1. The following details are displayed correctly in the Home page:
- Account Number
- Available Balance
- 3 Transaction History of the Account
2. Tapping on "Hide my balance" should mask the available balance.
3. The ff details are displayed:
Account Details
- Product Name
- Account Type
- Account Number

Interest Configuration
- Interest rate
- Interest type
- Interest time period
- Interest rate structure
4. The "Switch Acct" button should not be visible.
column14: Home screen elements not found within timeout.

column2: Accessing Product List

column6:
1. The Switch Acct button is visible.
2. When the Switch Acct button is clicked, the system displays the My Accounts modal containing list of all active products attached to the customer.
2. Each product displays its corresponding details:
- available/oustanding balance

column14: Switch Acct button check failed — login for multi-account user timed out.

column2: Verify switching to a different Savings product
column4:
1. User is logged into the mobile app.
2. Customer has at least two active savings accounts/product.
column5:
1. Tap on the "Switch Acct" button.
2. From the My Accounts modal, select a different savings account.
3. Tap on Hide my Balance
column6:
1. The UI smoothly transitions to the next account.
2. The following details are displayed correctly in the Home page:
- Account Number
- Available Balance
- 3 recent transactions
4. Tapping on "Hide my balance" should mask the available balance.
5. The system must strictly display only the information for the selected savings; it must not display transactions, balances, or configuration details belonging to any other savings product.
column14: Account switch did not complete — login timed out for AD_MULTI_EMAIL.
column15: Same root cause as m4.1.11 — AD_ACCT_NO_TEXT timeout on multi-account login.

column2: Verify data persistence when switching accounts with "Hide Balance" enabled
column4:
1. User is logged into the mobile app.
2. Customer has multiple accounts.
column5:
1. Tap "Hide my balance" on Account A.
2. Switch to Account B.
3. Observe the balance state.
column6:
1. The balance for Account B should remain masked (the "Hide" state should persist across account switches).
2. Tapping "Show balance" should reveal the correct balance for Account B only.
column14: Balance mask persistence check failed — login timed out for AD_MULTI_EMAIL.

column2: Verify behavior when switching to an account with zero transactions
column4:
1. User is logged into the mobile app.
2. Customer has a secondary account with no transaction history.
column5: 1. Switch from the primary account (with transactions) to the empty secondary account.
column6:
1. The account details (Number/Balance) update correctly.
2. The transaction section displays a placeholder message: 
"No Transactions Yet
Your transaction history will display here when you start using your account" instead of showing data from the previous account.
column15: No zero-transaction account available for AD_MULTI_EMAIL. Run manually with suitable test account.

column2: Verify system stability during rapid/consistent account switching
column4: 1. User has 3 or more active products (Savings and Loans).
column5:
1. Tap "Switch Acct" and select Account A.
2. Immediately tap "Switch Acct" again and select Account B.
3. Repeat switching rapidly 5-10 times.
column6:
1. The app remains stable without crashing.
2. The UI does not display "flickering" data (showing Account A's balance while on Account B's screen).
3. The final account selected displays the correct, synchronized data.
column14: Rapid account switching failed — login timed out.

column1: m4.2 As a User, I want to view my transaction history

column2: Verify that the user can access transaction history
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has 1 or more transactions.
column5:
1. Tap the “History” on the footer navigation bar.
2. Scroll down to view the transaction history
column6:
1. The user is redirected to the Transaction History page.
2. Transactions are filtered per month, showing only those within the last 30 calendar days.
3. Each group of transactions is preceded by a month header in the format "mmm yyyy" (e.g., Aug 2025).
4. Each transaction displays Account name, Transaction Amount, Bank, Masked Account number and a Timestamp in the format "hh:mm AM/PM".
5. Transactions are sorted by date in descending order (most recent first).
column15: Requires account switch from multi to single account after prior test — run manually.

column2: Verify that a maximum of 100 transactions are displayed in the transaction history
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has more than 100 transactions in the last 30 calendar days.
column5:
1. Tap the “History” icon on the footer navigation bar.
2. On the Transaction History page, continuously scroll down to load more transactions.
column6:
1. The user is redirected to the Transaction History page.
2. The system retrieves and displays transactions in increments of 10, up to a maximum 3. of 100 transactions.
4. Transactions are filtered per month, showing only those within the last 30 calendar days.
5. Each group of transactions is preceded by a month header in the format "mmm yyyy" (e.g., Aug 2025).
4. Each transaction displays Account name, Transaction Amount, Bank, Masked Account number and a Timestamp in the format "mm dd yyyy, hh:mm AM/PM".
7. Transactions are sorted by date in descending order (most recent first).
8. No additional transactions are loaded after the 100th transaction, even if more exist in the backend.

column2: Verify access to transaction history from the bank details page
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has 1 or more transactions.
column5:
1. Tap the “i” icon on the bank balance section of the home page.
2. Scroll to view the last three (3) transactions.
3. Tap the button below the last three account transaction records
column6:
1. The user is redirected to the full Transaction History page.
2. All transactions related to the selected account are displayed.
column15: View More navigation causes stale element issues — run manually.

column2: Verify that the user can view full transaction details
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has 1 or more transactions.
column5:
"1. Tap the “History” on the footer navigation bar.
2. Scroll down to view the transaction history"
3. Tap on a listed transaction.
column6:
1. The user is redirected to the Transaction Details page.
2. The following fields are shown clearly:
If Send Money:
- To: Receiver Name and Account Number
- Receiver's bank
- Date and Timestamp
- Transaction ID
- Instapay Reference Number (If extenal send)
- Notes

If Receive Money:
- From: Sender Name
- Sender's Bank
- Date and timestamp
- Transaction ID
- Transaction ID
- Instapay Reference Number (If extenal send)
- Notes
column14: Full transaction details screen loads with all required fields.

column2: Verify behavior when there are no transactions to show
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has no recorded transactions on their account.
column5:
1 Tap the “History” button in the footer navigation.

Or tap the “i” icon to go to the bank details page, then tap “View More”.
column6:
1. The user is redirected to the Transaction History page.

2. The screen displays a centered message:

“No transactions available
Your transaction history will display here when you start using your account.”

3. No transaction records are shown.
column14: Empty state message displayed when account has no transactions.

column2: Verify filter functionality for date range within past 30 days
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has transactions older and newer than 3 months from today.
column5:
1. Tap the “History” button in the footer.
2. Use the filter option to select a custom date range (e.g., March 1–March 31).
3. Apply the filter.
column6:
1. Only transactions that fall within the selected range (within the last 3 months) are displayed.

2. Transactions outside the selected range are not shown.
column15: No filter button found in current app build — verify with Appium Inspector.

column2: Verify behavior when filtering beyond past 30 days limit
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user attempts to view transactions older than 3 months.
column5:
1. Go to the Transaction History page.
2. Attempt to filter/select a date range that extends beyond the past 3 months (e.g., 6 months ago).
column6: 1. The system prevents the user from selecting a date range that starts beyond the allowed limit.
column15: No filter button found in current app build — verify with Appium Inspector.

column2: Verify that the user can access and use the search bar in the transaction history
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has one or more transactions in their transaction history.
column5:
1. Tap the “History” on the footer navigation bar.
2. Tap on the Search bar at the top of the Transaction History page.
3. Type any input into the search bar.
column6:
1. The Search bar is visible and interactive.
2. The system accepts both numeric and alphanumeric inputs.
3. The transaction list dynamically filters based on the entered search keyword.
4. Matching transactions are displayed in real-time while typing.
column14: Search bar is visible and filters transactions in real-time.

column2: Search by Account Name
column4:
1. User is on Transaction History. 
2. A transaction with a known Account Name (e.g., "John Doe") exists.
column5: 1. Enter the account name into the search bar.
column6: 1. The list filters to show only transactions involving "John Doe".
column14: Transactions filtered by account name as expected.

column2: Search by Account Number
column4:
1. User is on Transaction History. 
2. A transaction with a known Account Number (e.g., "...12345") exists.
column5: 1. Enter the account number into the search bar.
column6: 1. The list filters to show only transactions involving Account Number "...12345".
column14: Transactions filtered by account number as expected.

column2: Search by Reference ID
column4:
1. User is on Transaction History. 
2. A transaction with a known Reference ID (e.g., "R-ABC987") exists.
column5: 1. Enter the reference ID into the search bar.
column6: 1. The list filters to show only the transaction with Reference ID "R-ABC987".
column15: Instapay Ref ID not visible in history list — requires manual lookup from Transaction Details.

column2: Search by Transaction Amount
column4:
1. User is on Transaction History. 
2. A transaction with a known Amount (e.g., "500.00") exists.
column5: 1. Enter the transaction amount into the search bar.
column6: 1. The list filters to show only transactions with an amount of "500.00".
column14: Transactions filtered by amount as expected.

column2: Search with No Results
column4: 1. User is on Transaction History.
column5: 1. Enter a random string (e.g., "XYZ123NOMATCH") into the search bar.
column6:
1. The list becomes empty. 
2. A "No transactions available" message is displayed.
column14: No-results message displayed for unmatched search term.

column2: Clear Search Bar
column4: 1. User is on Transaction History and has entered text into the search bar, filtering the list.
column5: 1. Delete inputs in the search bar
column6:
1. The search text is cleared. 
2. The transaction list reverts to the full, unfiltered list.
column14: Full transaction list restored after clearing search bar.

column1: m4.4 As a User, I want to copy and paste my account number

column2: Verify that the user can copy their account number
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has a valid account number displayed on the screen.
column5:
1. Navigate to the Home Menu.
1. Navigate to the account number screen at the top.
2. Tap the Copy icon next to the account number.
3. Paste the copied value into any text field (e.g., Notes app, input field).
column6:
1. The account number is successfully copied to the clipboard.
2. The pasted value exactly matches the displayed account number.
column15: Copy button not accessible via Android accessibility tree (React Native component without a11y attrs).

column2: Verify copy action behavior when the user taps the copy icon repeatedly very quickly
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user has a valid account number displayed on the screen.
column5: 1. Tap the Copy icon repeatedly very quickly.
column6: 1. Only one copy is made, no crashes or glitches.
column15: Copy button not accessible via Android accessibility tree (React Native component without a11y attrs).
